name: DEADBAND
description: Deadband block - creates a deadzone where output is zero for inputs within a range

exprs.description: exprs[0]=end of deadband, exprs[1]=start of deadband, exprs[2]=method

xmlExample:
<BasicBlock id="b1" parent="0:2:0" interfaceFunctionName="DEADBAND" blockType="c" dependsOnU="1" dependsOnT="0" simulationFunctionName="deadband" simulationFunctionType="C_OR_FORTRAN" style="DEADBAND">
  <ScilabString as="exprs" height="3" width="1">
    <data line="0" column="0" value="0.5"/>
    <data line="1" column="0" value="-0.5"/>
    <data line="2" column="0" value="1"/>
  </ScilabString>
  <ScilabDouble as="realParameters" height="1" width="2">
    <data line="0" column="0" realPart="0.5"/>
    <data line="0" column="1" realPart="-0.5"/>
  </ScilabDouble>
  <mxGeometry as="geometry" x="0.0" y="-10.0" width="40.0" height="40.0"/>
</BasicBlock>
anomalies: nmode=1 (has continuous modes for zero crossing detection)
realParameters contains 2 values but exprs has 3
exprs[2] method not stored in parameters but used for calculation
criticalRules: If input is between start and end: output = 0
If input > end: output = input - end
If input < start: output = input - start
nbZerosCrossing=2 indicates zero-crossing detection at both edges
exprs[2]=1: use model with zero-crossing (accurate at discontinuities)
commonUses: Eliminating noise around zero in signals
